revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蟻通神社 (田辺市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:蟻通神社 田辺市湊 Aritooshi-jinja 2012.8.22 - panoramio.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Tanabe|qid=Q304266|1=Tanabe, Wakayama}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.733028|N|135.380778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}|architecture_style={{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}}}
'''Aritōshi Shrine''' (蟻通神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Minato, Tanabe City|ja|湊 (田辺市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato, Tanabe City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[Tanabe City]], {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|qid=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}<ref name="rekishi_sanpo">{{Cite book|和書|title=和歌山県の歴史散歩|author=和歌山県高等学校社会科研究協会|publisher=山川出版社|year=2009|page=212|isbn=4634246309|qq=}}</ref>. == Deities == ; Main enshrined deity : [[Ame-no-Koyane]] (天児屋根命)<ref name="rekishi_sanpo"/><ref name="jinjashi"/> ; Additional deities : Nishinomiya Ōkami, [[Ichikishima-hime|Ichikishima-hime]], {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Okunaga-tarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}, [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagu-tsuchi]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi-no-Ōkami]], [[Landlord deity|Jinushigami]], and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|qid=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}<ref name="jinjashi">{{Cite book|和書|title=和歌山県神社誌|author=神社誌編集委員会|publisher=和歌山県神社庁|year=2010|page=308|qq=}}</ref>. == History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|766|en|766|qid=Q30564|lt=766|1=766}} (the first year of {{ill|Tenpyō-jingo|en|Tenpyō-jingo|qid=Q1373181|lt=Tenpyō-jingo|1=Tenpyō-jingo}}), originally known as the shrine of the '''Goryō (御霊) and Gozu Tennō (牛頭天王)'''. It was renamed '''Aritōshi Myōjin Shrine''' in {{ill|1812|en|1812|qid=Q6933|lt=1812|1=1812}} (9th year of {{ill|Culture|en|Culture|qid=Q11042|lt=Bunka|1=Culture}}) and then changed to its current name, '''Aritōshi Shrine''', in {{ill|1868|en|1868|qid=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}} (1st year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji|1=Meiji era}})<ref name="jinjashi"/>. In {{ill|1873|en|1873|qid=Q7757|lt=1873|1=1873}} (6th year of Meiji), it was designated as a village shrine of Minato Village, Nishimuro District. In {{ill|1877|en|1877|qid=Q7773|lt=1877|1=1877}} (10th year of Meiji), {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|en|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|qid=Q713021|lt=Atago Shrine|1=Atago Shrine (Kyoto)}} was enshrined. In {{ill|1903|en|1903|qid=Q2045|lt=1903|1=1903}} (36th year of Meiji), {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|qid=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}} and Sumiyoshi Shrine were enshrined, followed by Hachiman Shrine in {{ill|1910|en|1910|qid=Q2075|lt=1910|1=1910}} (43rd year of Meiji)<ref name="jinjashi"/>. == Grounds == ; Main hall : Built in [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style, covering an area of 3.3 square meters<ref name="jinjashi"/>. ; Worship hall : Built in {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|qid=Q855025|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} style, covering an area of 39.7 square meters<ref name="jinjashi"/>. ; Shrine gate : Constructed in karahafu-zukuri style<ref name="jinjashi"/>. === Subordinate shrines === ; Nishinomiya Ōkamigū ; Benzaiten Shrine ; Sub-shrines within the main hall : Hachiman Shrine, Atago Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine<ref name="jinjashi"/>. ; Kusunoki Shrine ; Tenmangū == Cultural Properties == === Municipal Natural Monuments === * The forest of Aritōshi Shrine - Designated as a natural monument of Tanabe City on October 31, {{ill|1970|en|1970|qid=Q2474|lt=1970|1=1970}} (45th year of {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|qid=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}})<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=田辺市の指定文化財一覧 －記念物－|url=http://www.city.tanabe.lg.jp/bunshin/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_kinenbutsu.html|publisher=Tanabe City|accessdate=2017-09-04|qq=}}</ref>. == Annual Events == ; Minato Festival : Starting with the hōkoku-sai on October 1 and culminating with the annual festival on October 17, various events are held, including the dedication of lion [[kagura]] dances and the procession of [[mikoshi]] (portable shrines)<ref name="jinjashi"/>. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}}
== See Also == * [[Aritōshi]] * [[Aritōshi Myōjin]] * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|qid=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} {{Shinto navbox|qq=}}
{{Shinto-stub|qq=}} {{Wakayama-prefecture-stub|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub|qq=}}
